title: cuscuta campestris
intro: Cuscuta campestris, also known as field dodder, is a parasitic plant that derives its nutrients by connecting itself to the vascular system of a host plant. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta campestris include the potential to improve fertility, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases, such as rheumatism and eczema. Cuscuta campestris can be prepared as an herbal tea, extract, or capsule, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
